title: fix: neutralize account CSV formulas
author: its3li
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Neutralizes spreadsheet-formula prefixes in account CSV exports by prefixing risky cells with an apostrophe before normal CSV quoting.
- Adds regression coverage for detailed usage CSV and developer earnings CSV formula payloads.

## Security impact
User-controlled fields such as API key names / app names can appear in account CSV exports. Without neutralization, values beginning with `=`, `+`, `-`, `@`, tabs, or newlines may be interpreted as formulas when opened in spreadsheet software.

## Verification
- `npx biome check --write enter.pollinations.ai/src/routes/account.ts enter.pollinations.ai/test/account-usage.test.ts enter.pollinations.ai/test/account-earnings.test.ts`
- `npx biome check enter.pollinations.ai/src/routes/account.ts enter.pollinations.ai/test/account-usage.test.ts enter.pollinations.ai/test/account-earnings.test.ts`
- `npx vitest run test/account-usage.test.ts test/account-earnings.test.ts` from `enter.pollinations.ai` (9 passed)

Note: `npm run test --workspace pollinations-enter -- test/account-usage.test.ts test/account-earnings.test.ts` could not start here because the wrapper requires local `sops`, which is not installed in this environment.

gist.headline: Safe Spreadsheets
gist.blurb: We've added an extra layer of polish to our CSV exports, ensuring that app names and API keys play nicely with your favorite spreadsheet software.
gist.summary: Neutralized spreadsheet-formula prefixes in account CSV exports by prefixing risky cells with an apostrophe to prevent formula injection.
gist.impact: User-controlled fields like API key names and app names will no longer be accidentally interpreted as executable formulas when opened in spreadsheet software.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee wearing a tiny inspector's hat, examining a spreadsheet. The Nomnom creature eating dangerous-looking math symbols (=, +, -).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny inspector's hat, examining a glowing spreadsheet on a retro monitor. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats dangerous-looking math symbols like equals and plus signs. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
